jokes.text: there are some crybabies out there-- religious types mostly-- who might be offended
jokes.setup: Warning about scary content in Halloween special
jokes.punchline: Dismissive characterization of religious viewers as 'crybabies'
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons meta-commentary on moral panic around TV content, well-executed but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: We advise religious viewers that the following program contains material that may be considered sacrilegious. If you are easily offended, we suggest you change the channel. After all, we wouldn't want anyone to get their feelings hurt. But if you stay, try not to be such a crybaby about it.

jokes.text: Come on. I dare ya. [ Clucking ] Chicken.
jokes.setup: Direct challenge to viewers to turn off TV
jokes.punchline: Making chicken sounds to taunt viewers into watching
jokes.explanation: Clever audience manipulation tactic with perfect comedic timing

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: We dare you to turn off your television right now.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: Bawk bawk bawk bawk bawk!

jokes.text: That's not a Bible. That's a book of carpet samples. Ooh, fuzzy.
jokes.setup: Homer swearing on what he claims is a Bible
jokes.punchline: It's actually carpet samples, and Homer is distracted by the texture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - mixing sacred and mundane, then getting distracted by texture. Classic character-driven humor

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I swear on this Bible, I'm telling the truth!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's not a Bible.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What? Of course it is. Why would I... Whoa, what is this texture? This is incredible. So soft... so... plush...

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Those are carpet samples, Homer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmmm... carpet samples...

jokes.text: Behold mighty Caesar... in all his glory! D'oh!
jokes.setup: Grand introduction of Homer in Caesar costume
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation undermines the grandeur
jokes.explanation: Classic use of Homer's 'D'oh!' to puncture pompous moment

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: I am Calliope, the muse of heroic poetry. No kiddin'.
jokes.setup: Marge's pretentious classical reference
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual, unimpressed response
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Marge's intellectualism and Homer's simplicity

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's a reference to Proust's 'In Search of Lost Time.' It's about the nature of memory and—

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah. Can we order pizza now?

jokes.text: Stupid party. Wish we was trick-or-treating.
jokes.setup: Adult Halloween party with classical costumes
jokes.punchline: Kid wants simple trick-or-treating instead
jokes.explanation: Straightforward kid perspective humor, functional but not special

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Hey, can we just go trick-or-treating like normal kids?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Trick-or-treating? Bart, we're at an adult party!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah, but all you guys are just standing around in togas and stuff. At least when I go trick-or-treating, people give me candy.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Bart, the point of a costume party is to dress up and socialize.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Socialize? Mom, you've been talking to the same potted plant for twenty minutes.

jokes.text: Yo, Mom. We haven't got the eyeballs yet.
jokes.setup: Marge doing spooky Halloween storytelling with props
jokes.punchline: Homer interrupts the mood by being literal about missing props
jokes.explanation: Homer's practical interruption of spooky atmosphere is well-timed

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: And then the ghost reached out with his cold, bony hand...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, where's the hand? You said he had a hand.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, it's supposed to be scary, not literal.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, you can't have a ghost without a hand. That's just a torso floating around.

jokes.text: Homer, you're ruining it. Yeah, well, it was an evil game.
jokes.setup: Marge complaining Homer ruined the game
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive non-sequitur about the game being evil
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish deflection when caught ruining things

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you ruined the game!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: The game was evil, Marge. Evil!

jokes.text: A doll... from hell!
jokes.setup: Lisa saying she knows a story about a boy and his doll
jokes.punchline: Dramatic escalation to 'from hell!'
jokes.explanation: Classic horror movie escalation technique done well

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I know a story about a boy and his doll.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: A boy and his doll from hell!

jokes.text: I didn't earn it. I don't need it. But if they miss one payment, I'll raise hell!
jokes.setup: Grampa explaining his government money source
jokes.punchline: Hypocritical stance about demanding unearned money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa characterization - entitled but honest about it

dialogue.speaker: Grampa Simpson
dialogue.line: The government owes me money for all my years of service! I demand my benefits!

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Dad, didn't you just say welfare recipients are lazy freeloaders?

dialogue.speaker: Grampa Simpson
dialogue.line: That's different! I earned my government money. Those other people are just taking handouts!

jokes.text: We sell forbidden objects from places men fear to tread. We also sell frozen yogurt, which I call frogurt.
jokes.setup: Mysterious occult shop with ominous description
jokes.punchline: Mundane frozen yogurt with silly portmanteau name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of mystical and mundane, 'frogurt' is memorably absurd

dialogue.speaker: Shop Owner
dialogue.line: Welcome to our mystical establishment. Beware the dark forces within these walls.

dialogue.speaker: Shop Owner
dialogue.line: But first, can I interest you in some Frogurt?

jokes.text: But it comes with a free frogurt. That's good. The frogurt is also cursed. That's bad. But you get your choice of topping. That's good! The toppings contain potassium benzoate. That's bad. Can I go now?
jokes.setup: Cursed object warning
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous good/bad escalation ending with Homer's impatience
jokes.explanation: One of the most famous Simpsons bits - perfect escalation structure with absurd ending. The rhythm is flawless.

dialogue.speaker: Shop owner
dialogue.line: This amulet is cursed.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Cursed? Pfft. How bad could it be?

dialogue.speaker: Shop owner
dialogue.line: Well, you'll have bad luck for seven years.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Seven years? That's not so bad.

dialogue.speaker: Shop owner
dialogue.line: Then a swarm of locusts will devour your crops.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I don't have crops.

dialogue.speaker: Shop owner
dialogue.line: Then they'll devour your house.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: My house! Okay, how much longer?

dialogue.speaker: Shop owner
dialogue.line: Then your firstborn will be taken from you.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: How long is this gonna take? I'm hungry.

jokes.text: That doll is evil, I tells ya. Evil! E-E-Evil! Grampa, you said that about all the presents. I just want attention.
jokes.setup: Grampa's dramatic warning about evil doll
jokes.punchline: Admission that he just wants attention
jokes.explanation: Great Grampa moment - self-aware about his attention-seeking behavior

dialogue.speaker: Grampa Simpson
dialogue.line: That doll is evil! I'm telling you, it's possessed by the spirit of a vengeful toymaker!

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: Grampa, how do you know that?

dialogue.speaker: Grampa Simpson
dialogue.line: Well, to be honest with you, I just want someone to pay attention to me.

jokes.text: scientists have announced that Springfield's air... is now only dangerous to children and the elderly. Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Environmental news about dangerous air
jokes.punchline: Homer celebrates because he's not in the at-risk groups
jokes.explanation: Dark environmental satire combined with Homer's selfishness - very well crafted

dialogue.speaker: TV News Anchor
dialogue.line: We interrupt this program to bring you this special report. A recent study shows that prolonged exposure to our city's toxic air can cause serious health problems.

dialogue.speaker: TV News Anchor
dialogue.line: Particularly at risk are the very young, the very old, and the very poor.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo! I'm not very young, very old, or very poor!

jokes.text: I'm Krusty the Clown, and I don't like you. I'm Krusty the Clown, and I'm going to kill you.
jokes.setup: Sweet doll message from earlier
jokes.punchline: Escalating to murder threat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from earlier sweetness, but fairly predictable horror trope

dialogue.speaker: Krusty doll
dialogue.line: Kill Krusty! Kill Krusty! Kill Krusty!

jokes.text: I'd say that the pressure's finally gotten to Dad, but what pressure?
jokes.setup: Homer claiming doll tried to kill him
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out Homer has no real pressures in life
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about Homer's stress-free existence, well-timed

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That doll tried to kill me!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, you don't have any real pressures in your life.

jokes.text: There goes the last lingering thread Of my heterosexuality.
jokes.setup: Homer being terrorized by doll
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and dramatic reaction
jokes.explanation: Absurdly dramatic and oddly specific response to fear - memorable but dated

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why you little! Why you little!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Aaahhh! It's got me! It's got me!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Someone call the coroner! I'm dead!

jokes.text: I was a fool to think anyone would want Nude photos of Whoopi Goldberg.
jokes.setup: Moe disposing of something in garbage
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and unlikely business venture
jokes.explanation: Random and specific enough to be memorable, fits Moe's sleazy character

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: I'm gonna start a business where I collect old chewing gum from under tables and sell it back to the customers.

jokes.text: Your doll is trying to kill my husband! Yes, I'll hold.
jokes.setup: Emergency call about murderous doll
jokes.punchline: Mundane hold music response to supernatural emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between supernatural crisis and bureaucratic mundanity

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Yes, hello? My son has been attacked by a murderous doll!

dialogue.speaker: 911 Operator
dialogue.line: *elevator music plays*

jokes.text: Yep, here's your problem. Someone set this thing to 'evil.'
jokes.setup: Complex supernatural horror story about killer doll
jokes.punchline: Simple mechanical explanation like it's a broken appliance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deconstruction of horror tropes - treating supernatural as mundane mechanical problem. Perfect economy.

dialogue.speaker: Repair man
dialogue.line: Yeah, looks like your doll here's got a faulty servo motor in the neck joint. That's why it's been turning around and strangling people. I'll tighten that up for ya, should run ya about thirty bucks.

jokes.text: Yeah, dogs like to bury old junk. Yeah, you stupid idiot.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining dog behavior
jokes.punchline: Doll agreeing and insulting Homer
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing doll is now friendly but still rude to Homer

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: See, when a dog does that, it means he's happy.

dialogue.speaker: Krusty doll
dialogue.line: Yeah, and when you do it, it means you're a big dummy.

jokes.text: Homer made me give him a sponge bath. [ Shudders ]
jokes.setup: Doll is now Homer's friend/servant
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortable intimate task creating mutual disgust
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor about the awkwardness of their new relationship

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Okay Krusty doll, time for your bath!

dialogue.speaker: Krusty doll
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! I'm a toy, pal!

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Come on, you're my best friend now. Best friends bathe together.

dialogue.speaker: Krusty doll
dialogue.line: This is crossing a line, Homer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Don't be shy. We're bonding!

dialogue.speaker: Krusty doll
dialogue.line: Get that soapy sponge away from me!

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Why are you making this so difficult?

dialogue.speaker: Krusty doll
dialogue.line: Why are YOU making this so weird?

jokes.text: Homer, I've coughed up scarier stuff than that.
jokes.setup: Homer's weak ghost story
jokes.punchline: Grampa's gross but dismissive comparison
jokes.explanation: Good Grampa line combining dismissiveness with gross-out humor

dialogue.speaker: Grampa Simpson
dialogue.line: That ain't nothin'. I once saw a ghost so ugly, it scared itself to death. Then it came back as a ghost of a ghost, which is just a bad smell.

jokes.text: That's a lie, and you know it! But I have seen a lot of movies.
jokes.setup: Marge saying Grampa has led an interesting life
jokes.punchline: Honest admission of boring life but lots of movie watching
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa moment - self-aware but finding alternative source of stories

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: You've led such an interesting life, Dad.

dialogue.speaker: Grampa
dialogue.line: Interesting? Marge, I've done nothing but sit in that chair and watch TV for the past 30 years.

dialogue.speaker: Grampa
dialogue.line: But boy, have I seen some movies.

jokes.text: I think women and seamen don't mix. We know what you think.
jokes.setup: Discussion about hiring a woman for sea voyage
jokes.punchline: Burns acknowledging Smithers' obvious gay subtext
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre with Burns' knowing response to Smithers' sexuality

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers, I've decided to hire a woman for the sea voyage. What do you think?

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: A woman, sir? I... well, I suppose that's a practical choice.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Yes, yes. Though I notice you seem rather relieved about this decision, Smithers.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Relieved, sir? I'm sure I don't know what you mean.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Of course you don't, Smithers. Of course you don't.

jokes.text: I wish we were going to Candy Apple Island. Candy Apple Island? What do they got there? Apes, but they're not so big.
jokes.setup: Carl wishing for different destination than Ape Island
jokes.punchline: Candy Apple Island also has apes, defeating the purpose
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - the 'better' place has the same problem, just smaller

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: I wish we were going to Candy Apple Island instead.

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Candy Apple Island has apes.

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Oh, so it does.

jokes.text: We wouldn't think Of going without the bait. Uh, that is, the bait-thing beauty. The bathing beauty. I covered that up pretty well.
jokes.setup: Burns accidentally revealing Marge is bait
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bad cover-up attempts with self-congratulation
jokes.explanation: Good Burns moment showing his bad social skills and self-delusion

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Simpson's wife makes excellent bait. I mean— excellent... bait... for... compliments. Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: A slip of the tongue! Nothing to see here. Smithers, lock the doors.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm a master of deception. Truly a criminal mastermind.

jokes.text: we wouldn't dream of sacrificing the blue-haired woman. Oh, well, isn't that-- Whoa!
jokes.setup: Translation claiming they won't sacrifice Marge
jokes.punchline: Marge starts to respond positively before being grabbed
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection gag but well-executed timing

dialogue.speaker: Translator
dialogue.line: They say they won't sacrifice you.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh, that's wonderful! I was so worried—

dialogue.speaker: Translator
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. They changed their minds.

jokes.text: D'oh! D'oh! D'oh!
jokes.setup: King Kong roaring dramatically
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation echoing through valley
jokes.explanation: Great integration of Homer's catchphrase into King Kong parody with echo effect

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Hey, Homer, cut it out. Come on, quit eating me.
jokes.setup: Giant Homer supposedly being threatening
jokes.punchline: Casual complaint like it's minor annoyance, not mortal danger
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor treating being eaten as a minor social faux pas

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Yeah, and he keeps blocking my TV!

jokes.text: Well, the ape's going to stand around for three hours or so. Then we'll close with the ethnic comedy Of Dugan and Dershowitz.
jokes.setup: Theater promoter asking about the show content
jokes.punchline: Hilariously boring premise followed by dated ethnic comedy act
jokes.explanation: Good satire of lazy entertainment and vaudeville era comedy

dialogue.speaker: Theater Promoter
dialogue.line: What's your show about?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Well, it's about a man who owns a nuclear power plant and has a son named Bart. Hilarious stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Theater Promoter
dialogue.line: That sounds terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Wait, there's more. We also have a trained chimpanzee doing impressions of various ethnic groups.

dialogue.speaker: Theater Promoter
dialogue.line: Even worse.

jokes.text: Wow! Look at the size Of that platform!
jokes.setup: Giant ape on stage for audience amazement
jokes.punchline: Audience impressed by wrong thing entirely
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke about missing the obvious spectacle

dialogue.speaker: Audience member
dialogue.line: Wow! Look at the size of that ape!

dialogue.speaker: Audience member
dialogue.line: And look at those perfectly aligned teeth!

jokes.text: Come on. What's he gonna do? Run amok in downtown Springfield?
jokes.setup: Someone worried about giant ape escaping
jokes.punchline: Burns dismissively describing exactly what's about to happen
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic irony but well-timed before the rampage

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Bah! A giant ape, you say? Nonsense. Even if one were to escape, it would simply climb to the top of the nearest structure, swat at a few biplanes, and fall to its death. Nothing to worry about.

jokes.text: I said one!
jokes.setup: Offering peanuts to giant ape
jokes.punchline: Indignant about portion control during monster attack
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist priorities during chaos - vendor integrity over safety

dialogue.speaker: Vendor
dialogue.line: Would you like to buy some peanuts?

dialogue.speaker: Vendor
dialogue.line: Only a nickel!

jokes.text: I'm dreading the reviews. I can tell you that.
jokes.setup: Giant ape rampaging through city
jokes.punchline: Burns worried about theater critics amid disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns priorities - ego and business concerns over human cost

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers! A giant ape is destroying the city!

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Yes, sir. What should we do?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Do? We must ensure the theater critics are safe. They're reviewing my new play tonight!

jokes.text: Maybe you should eat more vegetables and less people.
jokes.setup: Giant Homer looking unwell
jokes.punchline: Practical dietary advice treating cannibalism casually
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - practical domestic advice applied to monster situation

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you look terrible. What have you been eating?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I tried that new restaurant down by the dock.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's a seafood place. You're not supposed to eat the customers!

jokes.text: I remember when Al Jolson ran amok at the Winter Garden and climbed the Chrysler Building. After that, he couldn't get arrested in this town.
jokes.setup: King Kong style rampage
jokes.punchline: Treating it as show business history with career consequences
jokes.explanation: Clever historical reference treating monster rampage as entertainment industry mishap

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: We interrupt this program to bring you breaking news. A giant ape has escaped from the Springfield Zoo and is currently destroying downtown Springfield.

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: Authorities are urging all citizens to remain calm and stay indoors.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: A giant ape? Cool! I'm going downtown to see it!

dialogue.speaker: Marge Simpson
dialogue.line: Homer, no! It's too dangerous!

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Don't worry, Marge. I'll be fine. Besides, this is the most exciting thing that's happened in Springfield since that time the town was invaded by a giant ape.

dialogue.speaker: TV Announcer
dialogue.line: The giant ape has now climbed the Springfield Nuclear Power Plant. Experts say this could be the end of civilization as we know it.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers, is the ape covered by our insurance policy?

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: I'm afraid not, sir. Acts of giant apes are specifically excluded.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Blast! Oh well, at least this will make for good box office.

jokes.text: I can't find your father. Oh, Homer.
jokes.setup: Marge looking for Homer at their wedding
jokes.punchline: Homer has apparently eaten the father of the bride
jokes.explanation: Dark visual gag about Homer's eating habits ruining wedding

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer? Homer?! Where is Homer?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Has anyone seen my husband?

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Well, Marge, it appears your husband has... eaten the father of the bride.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Homer!

jokes.text: Folks, if you could just stop cleaning each other for a second.
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony with ape guests
jokes.punchline: Reverend dealing with ape grooming behavior
jokes.explanation: Absurd wedding logistics with animal behavior

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Do you take this woman to be your wife?

dialogue.speaker: Groom Ape
dialogue.line: *grooms bride's fur*

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: I'll take that as a yes. And do you—oh my, is he eating bugs out of her hair?

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Well, I now pronounce you husband and wife. You may groom the bride.

jokes.text: Now, kids, instead of candy... I thought I'd serve an array of healthy fresh fruits. Fruit is nature's candy.
jokes.setup: Halloween treat alternative
jokes.punchline: Marge's well-meaning but unpopular health choice
jokes.explanation: Classic parent health food delusion

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: This Halloween, instead of candy, I'm handing out sugar-free, gluten-free, non-GMO, locally-sourced, organic fruit leather!

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: But what about taste?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: It tastes like health!

jokes.text: Hey, hi, fellow Halloweenies. Did I scaredely-dareya?
jokes.setup: Ned trying to be scary for Halloween
jokes.punchline: Childish made-up word that's completely non-threatening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned characterization - trying to be edgy but remaining wholesome

dialogue.speaker: Ned Flanders
dialogue.line: Well, howdy-doodly, trick-or-treaters! I'm a big scary... Spooky-Wooky Monster-Palooza!

jokes.text: Nice try, Mr. Flanders, but I've got a story so scary... you'll wet your pants. Too late.
jokes.setup: Bart boasting about having scary story
jokes.punchline: Ned has already wet his pants from anticipation
jokes.explanation: Good Ned characterization showing his anxiety and Bart's effect on adults

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dude, I got this scary story that'll make your hair stand on end.

dialogue.speaker: Ned
dialogue.line: Oh, I-I don't know, Bart. I get awful squeamish.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Come on, Flanders. It's not even that bad.

dialogue.speaker: Ned
dialogue.line: Well... oh, I'm afraid I've already wet my pants just thinking about it.

jokes.text: Man, he's just not trying anymore.
jokes.setup: Looking at 'Find Waldo Yet Again' book
jokes.punchline: Commentary on franchise fatigue and declining effort
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on commercial franchises running out of steam

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Hey, where's Waldo in this one?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: According to the copyright date, they stopped trying three years ago.

jokes.text: Run over by the mayor's beer-swilling brother, Clovis.
jokes.setup: Explaining how Snowball I died
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific detail about mayor's alcoholic brother
jokes.explanation: Good Springfield world-building with specific corrupt family detail

jokes.text: Cullen. Rayburn. Narz. Trebek. Zabar, Kresge, Caldor, Wal-Mart!
jokes.setup: Reading magical incantation to raise dead
jokes.punchline: Mix of game show hosts and retail stores as spell components
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist spell mixing random cultural references in believable pattern

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Spirits of the netherworld, I summon thee with the ancient words of power: Combine the essence of Bob Barker, the mystical energy of Vanna White, the dark magic of Kmart, and the unholy power of Sears!

jokes.text: Please, Lise, they prefer to be called 'the living impaired.'
jokes.setup: Lisa calling them zombies
jokes.punchline: PC language correction for undead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of political correctness applied to horror movie monsters

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: They're not zombies, they're the undead.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Whoa, chill out Lisa. Nobody cares about PC language.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: It's not about being politically correct, Bart. It's about respect for the undead community.

jokes.text: Did you wreck the car? No. Did you raise the dead? Yes! But the car's okay? Uh-huh. All right, then.
jokes.setup: Kids confessing to raising zombie apocalypse
jokes.punchline: Homer only cares about car being undamaged
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - material concerns over supernatural crisis. Excellent structure.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad, we accidentally raised the dead and started a zombie apocalypse.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Is my car okay?

jokes.text: Homer, did you barricade the door? Why? Oh, the zombies. No.
jokes.setup: Basic zombie survival question
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious delayed realization
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness to obvious danger

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, if there were a zombie apocalypse, would you know how to survive?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Of course! I'd just do what I always do.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Which is?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hide in the basement and eat all the food.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's not survival, that's just a Tuesday.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait... you mean there wouldn't be any zombies on a regular Tuesday?

jokes.text: Dad, you killed the Zombie Flanders. He was a zombie?
jokes.setup: Homer killing zombie Ned
jokes.punchline: Homer didn't realize Ned was a zombie, killed him anyway
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's relationship with Ned - would kill him regardless

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Take that, zombie Ned!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, he was already dead.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, problem solved!

jokes.text: Wow, George Washington! Take that, Washington! Eat lead, Einstein. Show's over, Shakespeare! Is this the end of Zombie Shakespeare?
jokes.setup: Homer fighting historical figure zombies
jokes.punchline: Shakespeare's dramatic death speech as zombie
jokes.explanation: Absurd zombie historical figures with Shakespeare staying in character

dialogue.speaker: Shakespeare
dialogue.line: To be or not to be, that is no longer the question! For I am undead, and must feast upon thy brains!

jokes.text: I'm not a zombie. But hey-- when in Rome.
jokes.setup: Barney eating brains with zombies
jokes.punchline: He's not infected, just going along with crowd
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney characterization - going along with any group activity, even cannibalism

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: He's not infected, just going along with the crowd.

jokes.text: Still pushin' that boulder? Uh-huh.
jokes.setup: Meeting mythological figure in afterlife
jokes.punchline: Casual conversation about eternal punishment
jokes.explanation: Casual treatment of classical mythology punishment

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: So you're Sisyphus? I heard you gotta push that boulder up a hill forever.

dialogue.speaker: Sisyphus
dialogue.line: Yes, it is my eternal curse.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Huh. Well, at least you get some exercise. I just sit on my couch and my boulder pushes me around.

dialogue.speaker: Sisyphus
dialogue.line: That does sound rather unpleasant.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Eh, could be worse. At least I'm not in hell or anything.

jokes.text: Well, I'm sure glad we didn't turn into mindless zombies. Shh! TV. Man fall down. Funny.
jokes.setup: Marge relieved they avoided becoming mindless zombies
jokes.punchline: Family already acting like mindless TV zombies
jokes.explanation: Excellent ending joke about TV turning people into zombies anyway - perfect ironic structure

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I'm so glad we avoided becoming mindless zombies.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, that would be terrible. Now come on, the TV's on.
